cases.passage: The Indian government has been conducting a nationwide survey to assess the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the country's workforce. According to the survey, the average age of the workforce in the urban areas is 35.6 years, with a standard deviation of 8.2 years. In the rural areas, the average age is 32.9 years, with a standard deviation of 6.5 years. Compare the age distribution of the workforce in urban and rural areas.

questions.q: What is the average age of the workforce in the urban areas?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the average age of the workforce in urban areas is 35.6 years.

questions.q: What is the standard deviation of the workforce age in rural areas?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the standard deviation of the workforce age in rural areas is 6.5 years.

questions.q: What can be inferred from the comparison of the average age of the workforce in urban and rural areas?
opts: (A) The workforce in urban areas is older than in rural areas
(B) The workforce in rural areas is older than in urban areas
(C) The workforce in urban and rural areas has the same age distribution
questions.explanation: The passage states that the average age of the workforce in urban areas (35.6 years) is higher than in rural areas (32.9 years).

questions.q: What type of data is presented in the passage?
questions.explanation: The passage presents data on the average age of the workforce, which is an interval data as it has a meaningful zero point.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the nationwide survey mentioned in the passage?
opts: (A) To assess the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the economy
(B) To compare the age distribution of the workforce in urban and rural areas
(C) To determine the average age of the workforce in the country
(D) To identify the standard deviation of the workforce age in urban areas
questions.explanation: The passage states that the survey is to assess the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the country's workforce, but the specific purpose of comparing the age distribution of the workforce in urban and rural areas can be inferred.

cases.passage: The Central Bureau of Statistics has released a report on the average monthly expenditure of households in India. According to the report, the average monthly expenditure of a two-person household is ₹ 18,500, with a standard deviation of ₹ 2,500. The average monthly expenditure of a three-person household is ₹ 22,500, with a standard deviation of ₹ 3,000. Compare the average monthly expenditure of two-person and three-person households.

questions.q: What is the average monthly expenditure of a two-person household?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the average monthly expenditure of a two-person household is ₹ 18,500.

questions.q: What is the standard deviation of the average monthly expenditure of a three-person household?
questions.explanation: The passage states that the standard deviation of the average monthly expenditure of a three-person household is ₹ 3,000.

questions.q: What can be inferred from the comparison of the average monthly expenditure of two-person and three-person households?
opts: (A) The monthly expenditure of three-person households is higher than that of two-person households
(B) The monthly expenditure of two-person households is higher than that of three-person households
(C) The monthly expenditure of two-person and three-person households is the same
questions.explanation: The passage states that the average monthly expenditure of a three-person household (₹ 22,500) is higher than that of a two-person household (₹ 18,500).

questions.q: What type of data is presented in the passage?
questions.explanation: The passage presents data on the average monthly expenditure of households, which is an interval data as it has a meaningful zero point.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the report released by the Central Bureau of Statistics?
opts: (A) To compare the average monthly expenditure of two-person and three-person households
(B) To determine the average monthly expenditure of households in India
(C) To identify the standard deviation of the average monthly expenditure of households
(D) To assess the impact of inflation on household expenditure
questions.explanation: The passage states that the report is on the average monthly expenditure of households in India, but the specific purpose of comparing the average monthly expenditure of two-person and three-person households can be inferred.
